basic_details.job_title: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentices Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 95 Graduate, Diploma & ITI Apprentices Posts
basic_details.short_information: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 has been officially announced by the ISRO Telemetry Tracking & Command Network (ISTRAC), Bengaluru under the Department of Space, Government of India. A total of 95 apprentice positions - across Graduate, Diploma, and ITI (Trade) categories - are open for one-year training under Advt. No. ISTRAC:01:2026 dated 22 June 2026. The last date to apply via email is 31 July 2026. Only candidates who passed their qualifying course in 2024, 2025, or 2026 are eligible.

application_fee.details: ISRO ISTRAC has not prescribed any application fee for this apprenticeship recruitment. The application process is free of cost for all candidates across all categories.

qualification.details: The ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026 eligibility rules are strict and candidates should verify each condition carefully before applying. Only those who passed their qualifying examination as regular candidates are eligible - correspondence, private, or sandwich course degrees are not accepted. Candidates currently pursuing higher studies (e.g., B.E./B.Tech for diploma holders) are also not eligible. | Graduate Apprentice (GA01-GA06): B.E./B.Tech in the relevant engineering discipline from any recognised University. For GA07 (Library Science): MLISc. (Library and Information Science). Diploma Apprentice (DA01-DA06): Diploma in Engineering awarded by a recognised State Board in the respective discipline. DA06 requires Diploma in Commercial Practice. ITI Trade Apprentice (TA01-TA05): ITI certificate in the concerned trade from any recognised University. | Candidates must have passed their qualifying course in 2024, 2025, or 2026 only. Candidates are also required to join within 3 years of acquiring the degree/diploma/ITI (calculated from the month and year of passing as stated in the certificate). If this three-year window lapses before the date of joining, the candidature will be cancelled. | Every candidate must register on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) portal at nats.education.gov.in before applying. The NATS Enrolment Number must be included in the email application. Applications without a valid NATS Enrolment Number are liable to be rejected. Candidates must upload their Degree/Diploma/ITI certificate and a valid government-issued ID on the NATS portal during registration. | Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Ex-apprentices, candidates currently undergoing apprenticeship elsewhere, or candidates with one year or more of work experience in Government/Public/Private Sector as on the date of joining are not eligible. Combining this apprenticeship with any other institution's apprenticeship is not permitted. Courses must be recognised by any University in India or State Boards.

salary_details.details: Candidates selected under ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 will receive a monthly stipend as fixed under the Apprentices Act. The stipend varies by apprentice category as follows: | Apprentices should note that there is no claim for regular employment in ISRO or any other Centre/Unit under the Department of Space after completion of the one-year training period. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: The ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice selection process consists of document scrutiny followed by an in-person interview. There is no written examination. Candidates shortlisted after screening will be called to the respective location for document verification and interview
and a merit panel will be drawn based on academic scores and interview performance.

Application Scrutiny: Submitted emails are screened for eligibility and completeness. Shortlisting: Eligible candidates are shortlisted based on academic percentage and other criteria. Document Verification / Interview: Shortlisted candidates are called to the respective centre. Interviews are held at Lucknow (27-28 August 2026)

and Bengaluru (09-11 September 2026). Reporting time at all centres is 8:30 AM. Panel Formation: A merit panel is prepared based on Degree/Diploma/ITI academic scores and interview performance. Final Induction: Apprentices are inducted against vacant positions strictly based on their rank in the panel. Selected candidates are informed only via email.

Important: Candidates shortlisted for document verification/interview are not eligible for Travelling Allowance (TA). No request to change the place of interview or place of posting will be entertained.
how_to_apply: Applications for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026 are accepted exclusively via email - no online portal, no postal applications. Candidates must send their application to the dedicated email ID of the location they are applying for. If applying to multiple locations, separate emails must be sent to each location's dedicated email address.
Register on the NATS portal at nats.education.gov.in and obtain your NATS Enrolment Number.

Prepare scanned copies of: Final Degree/Diploma/ITI Certificate (or final year/semester mark sheet if certificate is awaited), Class X and Pre-University marks card/certificate, and NATS Enrolment Number.
Compose an email to the correct location-specific email ID (see table below).
In the Subject Line, mention the Post Code - e.g., "Application for Post Code: GA02".
In the body of the email, clearly state: Name, Contact Number, Degree/Diploma/ITI Details, Year of Passing, and NATS Enrolment ID.
Attach all required documents and send before 31 July 2026.

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Notification Out
content_sections.text: Official Notification PDF - ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026 Download Here

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentices Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates should note these critical dates before applying for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026. The notification was issued on 22 June 2026, and the email application window closes on 31 July 2026. Interview dates have already been scheduled location-wise for August-September 2026.

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentices Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Breakdown
content_sections.text: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026 vacancies are distributed across three apprentice categories and multiple engineering disciplines. Graduate Apprentices require a B.E./B.Tech degree, Diploma Apprentices require a state board diploma, and ITI Trade Apprentices require an ITI certificate in the notified trade. Location-wise distribution is given below.

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentices Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: The ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026 eligibility rules are strict and candidates should verify each condition carefully before applying. Only those who passed their qualifying examination as regular candidates are eligible - correspondence, private, or sandwich course degrees are not accepted. Candidates currently pursuing higher studies (e.g., B.E./B.Tech for diploma holders) are also not eligible. Graduate Apprentice (GA01-GA06): B.E./B.Tech in the relevant engineering discipline from any recognised University. For GA07 (Library Science): MLISc. (Library and Information Science). Diploma Apprentice (DA01-DA06): Diploma in Engineering awarded by a recognised State Board in the respective discipline. DA06 requires Diploma in Commercial Practice. ITI Trade Apprentice (TA01-TA05): ITI certificate in the concerned trade from any recognised University. Candidates must have passed their qualifying course in 2024, 2025, or 2026 only . Candidates are also required to join within 3 years of acquiring the degree/diploma/ITI (calculated from the month and year of passing as stated in the certificate). If this three-year window lapses before the date of joining, the candidature will be cancelled. Every candidate must register on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) portal at nats.education.gov.in before applying. The NATS Enrolment Number must be included in the email application. Applications without a valid NATS Enrolment Number are liable to be rejected. Candidates must upload their Degree/Diploma/ITI certificate and a valid government-issued ID on the NATS portal during registration. Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Ex-apprentices, candidates currently undergoing apprenticeship elsewhere, or candidates with one year or more of work experience in Government/Public/Private Sector as on the date of joining are not eligible. Combining this apprenticeship with any other institution's apprenticeship is not permitted. Courses must be recognised by any University in India or State Boards.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: ISRO ISTRAC has not prescribed any application fee for this apprenticeship recruitment. The application process is free of cost for all candidates across all categories.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply - Email Only
content_sections.text: Applications for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026 are accepted exclusively via email - no online portal, no postal applications. Candidates must send their application to the dedicated email ID of the location they are applying for. If applying to multiple locations, separate emails must be sent to each location's dedicated email address. Register on the NATS portal at nats.education.gov.in and obtain your NATS Enrolment Number. Prepare scanned copies of: Final Degree/Diploma/ITI Certificate (or final year/semester mark sheet if certificate is awaited), Class X and Pre-University marks card/certificate, and NATS Enrolment Number. Compose an email to the correct location-specific email ID (see table below). In the Subject Line , mention the Post Code - e.g., "Application for Post Code: GA02" . In the body of the email , clearly state: Name, Contact Number, Degree/Diploma/ITI Details, Year of Passing, and NATS Enrolment ID. Attach all required documents and send before 31 July 2026 .

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentices Recruitment 2026- Selection Process
content_sections.text: The ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice selection process consists of document scrutiny followed by an in-person interview. There is no written examination. Candidates shortlisted after screening will be called to the respective location for document verification and interview, and a merit panel will be drawn based on academic scores and interview performance. Application Scrutiny: Submitted emails are screened for eligibility and completeness. Shortlisting: Eligible candidates are shortlisted based on academic percentage and other criteria. Document Verification / Interview: Shortlisted candidates are called to the respective centre. Interviews are held at Lucknow (27-28 August 2026), Sri Vijaya Puram (03-04 September 2026), and Bengaluru (09-11 September 2026). Reporting time at all centres is 8:30 AM. Panel Formation: A merit panel is prepared based on Degree/Diploma/ITI academic scores and interview performance. Final Induction: Apprentices are inducted against vacant positions strictly based on their rank in the panel. Selected candidates are informed only via email. Important: Candidates shortlisted for document verification/interview are not eligible for Travelling Allowance (TA) . No request to change the place of interview or place of posting will be entertained.

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentices Recruitment 2026 - Stipend / Monthly Salary
content_sections.text: Candidates selected under ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 will receive a monthly stipend as fixed under the Apprentices Act. The stipend varies by apprentice category as follows: Apprentices should note that there is no claim for regular employment in ISRO or any other Centre/Unit under the Department of Space after completion of the one-year training period. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

content_sections.heading: Interview Venue Details
content_sections.text: Candidates must report to the centre corresponding to the location they applied for. No request for change of interview venue will be accepted at any stage. The complete reporting addresses are:

rows.address: ISTRAC, Lucknow, Sector-G, Janakipuram, Kursi Road, Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh - 226021
ISTRAC, Sri Vijaya Puram, Dollygunj, Sri Vijaya Puram, Andaman and Nicobar Islands - 744103
ISTRAC, Bengaluru, Plot 12 & 13, 3rd Main, 2nd Phase, Peenya Industrial Area, Bengaluru - 560058

content_sections.heading: Documents Required at Interview
content_sections.text: Candidates called for document verification must carry all original documents to the venue. Failure to produce any of the required originals will result in disqualification from the apprenticeship training. Proof of date of birth Class X and Pre-University marks card/certificate Final Degree/Diploma/ITI Certificate or final year mark sheet (if certificate is awaited) Mark sheets of all years/semesters of qualifying course NATS Enrolment ID Caste/Tribe/Disability certificate in prescribed format (if applicable) Medical Fitness Certificate (to be submitted at the time of joining)

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting & Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Many eligible candidates get rejected due to avoidable errors in the email application. Review these common pitfalls carefully before sending your application to ISRO ISTRAC. Wrong email ID for location: Each training location has a separate email ID. Sending your Bengaluru application to the Lucknow email ID will result in rejection. Double-check the email address against the location you are targeting. Missing Post Code in subject line: The subject line must clearly mention the Post Code (e.g., "Application for Post Code: GA02"). Emails without the Post Code may not be processed. No NATS Enrolment Number: Registration on nats.education.gov.in is mandatory before applying. Not including the NATS Enrolment Number in the email body is grounds for rejection. Passing year outside 2024-2026: If you passed your qualifying course before 2024, you are not eligible regardless of your marks or experience. Applying with a private or sandwich course degree: Degrees obtained via private mode, correspondence, or sandwich courses are explicitly not accepted. Only regular candidates qualify. Currently pursuing higher studies: Diploma holders currently doing B.E./B.Tech, or graduates currently doing M.E./M.Tech, are not eligible. One year or more of work experience: Candidates with one year or more of work experience in Government, Public, or Private Sector as on the joining date are not eligible. Applying to multiple locations in one email: Send separate emails to separate location-specific IDs. Do not combine locations in a single email.

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Apprentices Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
content_sections.text: Applications for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 are accepted via email only - there is no separate online application portal. Candidates must email the relevant location-specific address before 31 July 2026 . NATS registration at nats.education.gov.in is mandatory before applying.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: ISRO ISTRAC has announced approximately 95 apprentice vacancies across Graduate, Diploma, and ITI Trade categories under Advt. No. ISTRAC:01:2026 dated 22 June 2026. Positions are distributed across training locations at Bengaluru, Lucknow, and Sri Vijaya Puram in disciplines including Computer Science, Electronics & Communication, Electrical, Mechanical, Civil, and Commercial Practice.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the email application for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is 31 July 2026. Applications must be sent to the dedicated email ID of the respective training location before this date.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a B.E./B.Tech (Graduate Apprentice), Diploma in Engineering from a recognised State Board (Diploma Apprentice), or ITI certificate (Trade Apprentice) in the relevant discipline. Crucially, candidates must have passed in 2024, 2025, or 2026 only. Only regular candidates are eligible — private, correspondence, and sandwich course qualifications are not accepted.

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: No. ISRO ISTRAC has not prescribed any application fee for this apprenticeship recruitment. All candidates can apply free of cost.

faq.question: Q5. What is the monthly stipend for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentices?
faq.answer: Graduate Apprentice Trainees will receive ₹12,300/month, Diploma Apprentice Trainees will receive ₹10,900/month, and ITI Trade Apprentice Trainees will receive ₹9,600/month during the one-year training period.

faq.question: Q6. How to apply for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Applications are accepted via email only. Candidates must first register on the NATS portal (nats.education.gov.in), then send an email with the Post Code in the subject line and all required details and documents in the body/attachments to the location-specific email ID — [email protected] (Bengaluru), [email protected] (Lucknow), or [email protected] (Sri Vijaya Puram).

faq.question: Q7. Is there a written test for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: No written exam is conducted. Selection is based on shortlisting of email applications followed by document verification and an interview. The final merit panel is drawn based on academic percentage in the qualifying degree/diploma/ITI and performance in the interview.

faq.question: Q8. When and where will the interviews be held?
faq.answer: Interviews will be held at ISTRAC Lucknow on 27–28 August 2026, at ISTRAC Sri Vijaya Puram on 03–04 September 2026, and at ISTRAC Bengaluru on 09–11 September 2026. Reporting time at all three centres is 8:30 AM. No request to change the interview venue will be entertained.

faq.question: Q9. What is the training duration for ISRO ISTRAC Apprentices?
faq.answer: The training period is 12 months for all categories — Graduate, Diploma, and ITI Trade Apprentices — commencing from the execution of the Apprenticeship contract as per the Apprentices Act 1961.

faq.question: Q10. Will ISRO offer a job after the apprenticeship training?
faq.answer: No. Apprentices will have no claim whatsoever for regular employment in ISRO or any other Centre/Unit of ISRO or the Department of Space after completion of the one-year training period. The apprenticeship is purely a training engagement under the Apprentices Act.
